title: Sweet almond filo triangles
subtitle: Crisp filo pastry parcels filled with almond paste and orange blossom water.
excerpt: Sweet almond filo triangles are small crisp parcels of filo pastry filled with a simple almond paste. Each triangle is made from one strip of buttered filo folded flag-style around a spoonful of filling. They bake in 15 minutes. The technique is the most useful filo skill to know: one strip, one fold, one triangle. Makes about 24.
sourceNotes: Filo triangles filled with nuts appear across Middle Eastern, North African, and Greek baking traditions, from baklava-type structures to individual parcels. The flag-fold technique (folding a strip of pastry diagonally to create a triangle) is used in both professional patisserie and home baking to create neat, sealed parcels. Sweet almond fillings are common across Moroccan, Lebanese, and Greek traditions. This recipe synthesises the standard triangle technique with a Moroccan-influenced filling using orange blossom water.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the assembled unbaked triangles on a tray, then bag. Bake from frozen at 180°C fan for 18 to 20 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: The recipe doubles easily. Make a larger batch of filling and filo strips, then work through them in batches.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The almond filling keeps in the fridge for three days. Assembled triangles are best baked fresh.

recipeTools.notes: Two flat trays lined with baking paper.

content.text: The flag-fold is the key technique. Take a long strip of buttered filo, place a teaspoon of filling at one end, then fold the corner over diagonally to form a triangle. Keep folding the triangle over itself along the strip, alternating left and right, until you reach the end. Tuck in the last flap. Each triangle takes about 30 seconds once you have the rhythm.
Keep the filo covered with a damp tea towel while you work. It dries and cracks in minutes when exposed to air.

items.prepNote: kept covered with a damp cloth

content.text: Mix {{almond-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}}, {{orange-blossom-water}}, and {{cinnamon-ground}} in a bowl. Add just enough beaten {{eggs}} to form a firm, rollable paste. It should hold together without being wet.
Heat the oven to 180°C fan (200°C conventional). Line two baking trays with baking paper.
Take one sheet of {{filo-pastry}} and cut into three long strips, each about 8 cm wide. Keep all other sheets covered.

Brush one strip with melted {{unsalted-butter}}. Place a heaped teaspoon of filling at the near end of the strip, slightly left of centre. Fold the right corner of the filo diagonally over the filling to form a triangle. Fold the triangle straight up, then diagonally again, and so on until you reach the end of the strip. Tuck the final loose end underneath. Place on the tray and brush with butter.
Repeat with the remaining strips and filling.
Bake for 13 to 15 minutes until deep golden and crisp. Cool on the trays for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack. Dust with {{icing-sugar}} if using.

attrs.heading: Troubleshooting
attrs.intro: Speed is everything with filo work.

items.symptom: Filo cracking and tearing while folding.
items.fix: Keep all unused filo covered with a damp (not wet) cloth. Work with one strip at a time and keep all others covered. If a sheet tears, patch it by pressing the broken pieces together; it will not show through the layers.

items.cause: Too much filling, or loose end not tucked in.
items.fix: A heaped teaspoon per triangle is enough. Tuck the final end securely underneath before placing on the tray.
